title: Operator TUI autonomous Snake Chat control mode for E2E tests
purpose: Add a deterministic non-interactive mode for Snake Chat TUI control so simple and repeatable E2E tests can drive the TUI without human confirmation. The mode must be explicitly enabled, test-scoped, allowlisted, audited and isolated from normal interactive chat behavior.
goal: Provide a safe autonomous mode where scripted chat commands can switch views, toggle overlays, open artifacts, render Markdown/Mermaid views, pause/resume snake behavior and verify visible state changes without requiring a human. Normal interactive mode remains default-deny with confirmations for risky/ambiguous actions.

context.user_goal: Es muss einen Modus geben, in dem der Mensch nicht gebraucht wird, allein schon für einfache E2E Tests.
context.design_decision: Do not weaken the normal Snake Chat command policy. Add a separate automation_policy/test_policy mode selected by config/env/CLI for E2E. This policy can auto-confirm only explicitly allowlisted low-risk commands and must refuse shell/file-destructive operations.

modes.description: Default mode. Human-driven chat control. Default deny, confirmations possible.
Non-interactive test mode. Executes allowlisted safe commands without human confirmation. Intended for CI/local E2E only.
Optional future mode for controlled demos. Same dispatcher, stricter timeout and visible banner. Not required for MVP.

explicitly_out_of_scope: No shell execution from autonomous chat mode.
No destructive file operations from autonomous chat mode.
No network calls triggered only by a chat command unless explicitly mocked/test-scoped.
No arbitrary LLM-generated commands executed without deterministic parser/policy validation.
No autonomous mode enabled by default in normal user sessions.

milestones.title: Autonomous E2E policy and command execution
Safety diagnostics, docs and CI integration

tasks.title: Document autonomous Snake Chat E2E mode
tasks.description: Document why autonomous mode exists and how it differs from normal interactive mode.
acceptance_criteria: Docs define interactive_safe and autonomous_e2e modes.
Docs include a Mermaid sequence diagram for scripted chat command execution without human confirmation.
Docs state that autonomous_e2e is never the default for normal TUI sessions.
Docs list allowed initial commands and explicitly forbidden command categories.

tasks.title: Add chat-control mode config loader
tasks.description: Add config/env/CLI resolvable mode selection for chat-control behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports mode values interactive_safe and autonomous_e2e.
Default mode is interactive_safe.
Environment variable ANANTA_TUI_CHAT_CONTROL_MODE can select autonomous_e2e for tests.
Invalid mode values fail closed to interactive_safe or produce a clear startup error according to config strictness.
The active mode is visible in diagnostics/status when chat-control is enabled.

tasks.title: Expose autonomous mode through TUI CLI/test startup path
tasks.description: Ensure tests can start the TUI in autonomous_e2e mode without requiring manual key presses.
acceptance_criteria: TUI startup can receive chat-control mode from config/env/test harness.
When autonomous_e2e is active, a visible status/banner says autonomous E2E mode is active.
Normal startup without env/config remains interactive_safe.
Tests can instantiate the relevant state without launching a real terminal.

tasks.title: Implement autonomous_e2e policy profile
tasks.description: Add a policy profile that auto-allows only deterministic low-risk allowlisted actions during E2E tests.
acceptance_criteria: autonomous_e2e auto-allows only the configured safe command/action allowlist.
Unknown commands remain denied.
Actions marked destructive, shell, network, external-write or high-risk are denied even in autonomous_e2e.
Policy decision records mode=autonomous_e2e and auto_confirmed=true only for allowlisted low-risk actions.
A command outside allowlist returns a denial message suitable for E2E assertions.

tasks.title: Add command allowlist configuration for autonomous E2E
tasks.description: Make autonomous E2E command allowlist explicit and inspectable.
acceptance_criteria: Allowlist is defined by action ids, not raw free-form text only.
Example config includes the initial allowlisted commands/actions.
Allowlist can be narrowed in tests for negative-policy scenarios.
Allowlist cannot enable shell/destructive categories unless future code adds an explicit separate high-risk gate.

tasks.title: Add deterministic command result markers for E2E assertions
tasks.description: Return machine-checkable status markers in chat/control result messages during autonomous E2E mode.
acceptance_criteria: Successful autonomous commands produce a structured result marker such as control_result.status=ok and action_id.
Denied autonomous commands produce control_result.status=denied and reason_code.
Markers are readable in logs/test output without relying on OCR/screenshots.
Human-facing text remains concise and understandable.

tasks.title: Create JSON fixture format for scripted Snake Chat E2E commands
tasks.description: Define a simple fixture format for feeding commands and expected outcomes into the TUI test harness.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture supports initial mode, initial view, commands, expected result markers and expected final state.
Fixture can include timeouts per step.
Fixture supports negative assertions such as command denied.
Fixture is valid JSON and documented with an example.

tasks.title: Implement autonomous Snake Chat E2E runner
tasks.description: Add a runner that starts or simulates the Operator TUI chat-control path and feeds scripted commands without human input.
acceptance_criteria: Runner accepts a fixture path.
Runner forces or verifies ANANTA_TUI_CHAT_CONTROL_MODE=autonomous_e2e.
Runner feeds chat commands into the same ChatMixin/control parser path used by interactive mode.
Runner collects structured command result markers and final TUI state summary.
Runner exits non-zero on failed expected assertions.

tasks.title: Add basic autonomous E2E scenario for view and overlay control
tasks.description: Test the simplest no-human flow: help, list views, switch view, toggle overlay, return success markers.
acceptance_criteria: Test sends /help tui and asserts a successful control response.
Test sends /view list and asserts available views are reported.
Test sends /view diagnostics or /view markdown when available and asserts active view changes.
Test sends /overlay views on and asserts overlay-visible state.
Test does not require a real terminal, GPU, Mermaid CLI or human confirmation.

tasks.title: Add negative autonomous E2E policy scenario
tasks.description: Verify autonomous mode still denies commands outside the test allowlist.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies unknown command is denied.
Test verifies shell-like command is denied.
Test verifies destructive file-like command is denied.
Test asserts denial result marker and reason_code are present.

tasks.title: Add visible autonomous mode banner/status
tasks.description: Make it obvious when the TUI is running in autonomous E2E mode.
acceptance_criteria: Status/footer/diagnostics include 'AUTONOMOUS_E2E' or equivalent when active.
The mode indicator is absent in normal interactive_safe sessions.
E2E tests can assert the mode via state, not only visual text.
Mode indicator does not consume the two-line view switcher overlay rows.

tasks.title: Add audit assertions for autonomous commands
tasks.description: Ensure autonomous command execution remains auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Successful autonomous commands produce audit events containing mode, action_id and result status.
Denied autonomous commands produce audit events containing mode, reason_code and denied status.
Audit tests do not store secret/full document content by default.
Audit can be captured in memory for tests.

tasks.title: Add CI-safe command to run autonomous Snake Chat E2E tests
tasks.description: Expose a stable command for local and CI execution of the autonomous E2E path.
acceptance_criteria: A documented command such as make test-tui-chat-control-e2e or python scripts/e2e/snake_chat_control_e2e.py --fixture ... exists.
The command runs without terminal graphics, GPU, Mermaid, browser or network dependencies.
The command exits non-zero on failed assertions.
The command prints a concise summary of passed/failed steps.

tasks.title: Document safety boundary for autonomous mode
tasks.description: Explicitly document why autonomous E2E is safe enough for tests but not a general unrestricted agent mode.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain that autonomous_e2e is allowlist-based, not free-form autonomy.
Docs state that LLM-generated commands still pass deterministic parser and policy validation.
Docs define which action categories are forbidden in this mode.
Docs include at least one positive and one negative test example.

summary_notes: Autonomous E2E mode is required so simple UI/chat flows can be tested without a human.
This does not remove safety from normal interactive Snake Chat mode.
The mode is explicitly allowlist-based and still uses parser, policy and dispatcher validation.
E2E assertions should read structured command result markers/state, not OCR screenshots.

end_summaries.title: Expected result after completion
items: Snake Chat control can run in autonomous_e2e mode for CI/local tests.
Scripted commands can drive safe TUI actions without human confirmation.
Unsafe, unknown or destructive commands are still denied in autonomous mode.
E2E tests can assert structured command results and final TUI state.
Normal interactive sessions remain human-safe and do not default to autonomous mode.
